Making EDIBLE CHURROS using a KID’S TOY?! ✨Recipe: Ingredients: Graham Cracker Crust: • 1 cup water • ¼ cup unsalted butter, cubed • 1 tbsp granulated sugar • ¼ tsp salt • 1½ cup all-purpose flour • 2 large eggs • ½ tsp vanilla extract For the Cinnamon Sugar Coating: • ½ cup granulated sugar • ¾ tsp ground cinnamon Directions: 1. In a shallow dish, whisk together the sugar and cinnamon. Set aside. 2. In a medium saucepan over medium-high heat, bring the water, butter, sugar, and salt to a boil. Reduce heat to low and stir in the flour, mixing continuously until a smooth dough forms, about 1–2 minutes. Transfer to a mixing bowl and let cool for 5 minutes. 3. Add the vanilla extract and egg to the dough. Using an electric mixer, blend until fully combined and smooth. (It may look like it's separating at first—keep mixing, and it will come together!). 4. Transfer the dough to the toy. Pipe short 3–4-inch lengths onto a parchment-lined tray, using scissors or a knife to cut the ends cleanly. Let rest in the freezer for an hour. 5. In a large skillet, heat oil to 400°. Remove churros from freezer and place frozen dough into the hot oil. Fry until golden brown, about 3-5 minutes, flipping often. 6. Remove from oil with tongs and place on paper towel, then toss the churros in the cinnamon-sugar mixture until fully coated. 7. Serve immediately with chocolate ganache or warmed nutella for dipping, and enjoy!😊

As promised, this is my current scalp care routine! I previously didn’t think additional scalp care would do much for me, but the research behind the Pantene Abundant & Strong system (and all the research I’ve found on other scalp ingredients / products since) really changed my mind. Now I think about my scalp care routine as a proactive routine to keep my scalp + new hair growth in the healthiest state possible. This isn’t a routine that I think everyone *needs* to do, but for those who are also interested in promoting scalp health & improving hair retention, hopefully this gives you some steps to choose from that might help! Also- the results I’ve seen would only be from the scalp serums + shampoos alone because I haven’t been using the scalp device or red light helmet long enough to see any visible change yet. LMK if you want videos on my top recommendations for scalp serums + scalp focused shampoos next! Products Mentioned: 1. Pantene Abundant & Strong Serum 2. Neutrogena Hair Restore Serum  3. OGX Pro Growth + Peptide Shampoo 4. Dr Groot Scalp Revitalizing Hair Thickening Shampoo 5. DS Labs Scalp Activator - this was gifted with no obligation to review 6. Current Body Red Light Helmet - this was gifted with no obligation to review #scalpcare #hairroutine #haircareroutine #healthyhair

This routine is for anyone who is not dealing with extreme damage but wants over all glossier & softer hair this is the the exact routine I built for getting glass hair on fine hair but will work for the wavy curly hair girls too!!  the science: the flatten your cuticle lies, the more light it reflects that's what gives the illusion of glass. glossy hair isn't a product, it's the consistency of routine over time that’s keeping that cuticle smooth from prewash to final styling  it took me a while to figure out the system, but once I did, the results compounded and now I wanna share my favourite tips with you because this is the routine that actually for every  wash day. the 5 things I never skip: 1. always create a barrier before shampoo: hair oil or conditioner first. less friction = less cuticle damage = more shine 2. weekly hair mask. deep hydration is what keeps that cuticle flat and the shine consistent. 3. a glossing treatment every 3-4 weeks  this is the step that takes glossy to glass  4. leave-in conditioner every single wash. beats frizz and keeps the cuticle flat between washes. 5. the hair sandwich method oil before heat styling AND after. then always finish with a cool shot to lock the cuticle in place ✨ Hope these haircare tips help you guys!! #hair #haircare #glasshair #hairgloss

the hair care routine that keeps my hair healthy ✨    all products linked on my shopmy which is linked in my bio    here’s the exact timeframe i follow each week: • i oil my hair 1–3x a week • i wash my hair based on oiliness, usually about every 2 days. i don’t follow a strict wash schedule • i use either a milk treatment, protein treatment, hair mask, or hair gloss about once a week #haircare #hairtok #healthyhair #hairroutine #hairtreatment

As part of our second year at WesternU Dental School, we do peer-to-peer local anesthetic injections to prepare for clinic in 3rd year. The five injections we did today were: 1. Inferior Alveolar Nerve Block 2. Long Buccal Neeve Block 3. Nasopalatine Nerve Block 4. Greater Palatine Nerve Block 5. Local Infiltration #dentalstudent #dentalschool #dentist #dental #dentistry

Key Health Benefits of Pilates: Deep Core Strength: Pilates targets the deep abdominal muscles and pelvic floor, creating a strong "powerhouse" that supports the spine and protects the back. Improved Posture and Alignment: By strengthening neglected postural muscles and promoting better body awareness, it helps correct slouching and alignment issues. Increased Flexibility and Mobility: The exercises focus on lengthening muscles while strengthening them, which improves joint mobility and overall flexibility. Reduced Back Pain and Injury Prevention: It strengthens muscles around the spine and hips, helping to alleviate pain and lower the risk of injuries. Toned Muscles Without Bulk: Using eccentric contraction, Pilates develops long, lean muscle tone rather than building mass. Mental Well-being and Stress Relief: The focus on precise movement and breathing promotes relaxation, reduces cortisol levels, and enhances mental concentration. Accessible Low-Impact Exercise: Because it is low-impact, it is accessible to various fitness levels and provides a safe way to exercise #fyp #targetaudience #pilates #motivation #pilatesbyizzy

MACARONS ECONÓMICOS: Paso a paso para unos macarons perfectos en casa, con Harina de Trigo 🌾🥰 • Es hora de que te animes a probar de hacer macarons caseros. Siempre se dijo que son algo muy difícil de hacer, y por sobre todo, ya que llevan Harina de Almendras, son un producto muy costoso. Es por eso que me puse a probar varias recetas hasta llegar a esta con la clásica harina de trigo que compras en el supermercado. Salen hermosos, con una muy buena textura y vas a ver que no tienen tanto que envidiarle a los clásicos! 💪🏻 • 𝕀𝕟𝕘𝕣𝕖𝕕𝕚𝕖𝕟𝕥𝕖𝕤: • 3 Claras de Huevo 🥚 (aprox 100gr) • 100gr Azúcar Común  • 105gr Harina de Trigo para Reposteria 0000 🌾  • 105gr Azúcar Impalpable o Glas  • Colorante en Gel del color que quieran. Esto es opcional, ya que blancos con los rellenos de colores, salen hermosos también!  • 𝔾𝕒𝕟𝕒𝕔𝕙𝕖 𝕕𝕖 ℂ𝕙𝕠𝕔𝕠𝕝𝕒𝕥𝕖 𝔸𝕞𝕒𝕣𝕘𝕠: • 100gr Chocolate Amargo 🍫  • 100gr Crema de Leche (para Batir) 🥛  • 𝔾𝕒𝕟𝕒𝕔𝕙𝕖 𝕕𝕖 ℂ𝕙𝕠𝕔𝕠𝕝𝕒𝕥𝕖 𝔹𝕝𝕒𝕟𝕔𝕠 𝕪 𝕃𝕚𝕞𝕠𝕟: • 100gr Chocolate Blanco 🍫  • 75cc Crema de Leche 🥛  • Ralladura de 1/2 Limón 🍋  • Colorante Rojo a Gusto (Liposoluble) ❤️  • ¿Te animas a preparar este clásico de las pastelerías francesas en tu casa? Proba esta versión y después me contas 🫶 • #macarons #pasteleria #macaron
